revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Antoku Ōzuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Antoku Ōzuka Kofun | native_name =安徳大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Antoku Ōzuka Kofunn | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|53|N|130|25|52|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Fukuoka|en|Nakagawa, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206001|lt=Nakagawa, Fukuoka|1=Nakagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|29|53|N|130|25|52|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Antoku Ōzuka Kofun'''|安徳大塚古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of {{ill|Nakagawa, Fukuoka|en|Nakagawa, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206001|lt= Nakagawa|1=Nakagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245831|title=安徳大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Antoku Ōzuka Kofun is located on the tip of a west-facing hill with an elevation of about 30 meters, orientated to the west. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. In 1971, the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an [[archaeological excavation]] due to the development of a nearby housing complex. As a result, it was found that the total length of the mound was 64 meters, and that the tumulus was built by cutting a moat approximately 10 meter wide from the front and rear hills. The diameter of the posterior circular part is approximately 35 meters and the height is approximately six meters. The rectangular anterior portion is approximately 30 meters long, with a width of approximately 20 meters, and height of two meters. The slopes of both areas were covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The tumulus had been robbed in antiquity and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain. From the ''haniwa'' and pottery shards recovered at the site, together with the shape of the mound, it is estimated that this tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century, or early in the Kofun period.<ref name="Bunka1"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.nakagawa.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/2697.pdf Nakagawa city home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Antoku Ōzuka Kofun]]
